summary: Security and governance platform for enterprise AI agents and their runtime behavior.
editor_note: The bet is that AI-agent risk lives in what agents decide to do between prompt and output. Geordie maps agents across cloud, code, endpoint, tools, permissions, MCP connections, and runtime behavior; its Series A post says one POC surfaced 327% more agents than existing inventories. Unlike prompt firewalls, it is watching agent operations. The risk is whether security teams can enforce controls without slowing adoption.

product_evidence.claim: The product page says Geordie discovers agents across cloud, code, and endpoint environments and maps tools, permissions, MCP connections, skills, prompts, models, and sub-tools.
The Series A post describes Beam runtime remediation and says one customer POC found 327% more agents than existing inventories, plus MCP command injection and credential-leakage risks.

market_context.primary_user: Security and governance teams responsible for enterprise AI-agent adoption.
market_context.differentiation: Agent-native visibility and runtime controls rather than prompt-only or network-only guardrails.
market_context.why_now: The May 28, 2026 Series A followed RSAC Innovation Sandbox momentum and enterprise demand for AI-agent governance.

risks.claim: The risk is whether agent governance becomes a standalone buying category or gets absorbed into existing security suites.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product and funding-source review.
